chains.name: Tech

signals.source: USPTO patent filings by tracked companies

chains.conclusion: Tech chain shows mixed signals with a notable drop in academic research output (arXiv papers down 45% and Hacker News coverage down 35%), suggesting reduced near-term visibility in protein folding, drug discovery AI, and autonomous driving discussions. However, Crunchbase tech funding news surged 150% above average, indicating continued investor interest despite lower public discourse. This divergence suggests funding activity may be outpacing research publication cycles or shifting toward private announcements.

chains.name: Economy

signals.source: Job market proxy — Indeed RSS count

chains.conclusion: Economy chain shows no actionable signals. Yield spread remains stable at 0.49, within normal range. Port congestion and job market proxies remain at baseline zero levels. One source (cardboard box production) is silent. Overall economic indicators show no emerging trends or disruptions.

chains.name: Biotech

signals.source: NIH Reporter active grants this FY

chains.conclusion: Biotech chain shows divergent signals. NIH active grants rose 11% above average, suggesting sustained federal funding momentum for protein folding, CAR-T, mRNA, and CRISPR research. However, bioRxiv preprints dropped 22% below average and SEC S-1 biotech filings fell to zero (100% below average), indicating reduced near-term IPO activity and slower research publication velocity. This suggests funding is flowing but commercialization pipelines may be tightening.

chains.name: Social
chains.conclusion: Social chain shows declining public interest. Google Trends interest in autonomous vehicles, robotaxi, protein folding, and small modular reactors dropped 48% below the 30-day average, indicating reduced consumer and mainstream attention to these topics. Bluesky data is unavailable. Kickstarter and Amazon Movers metrics remain at baseline. Overall social signal momentum is weakening.

chains.name: Geopolitics

signals.source: % of tracked commodities above own 30d baseline

chains.conclusion: Geopolitics chain shows no emerging signals. Commodity tracking data is unavailable. Shipping congestion, EU consultations, and Congressional hearings all remain at baseline zero levels. No geopolitical disruptions or policy shifts are evident in the monitored signals.

chains.name: Corporate

signals.source: SEC Form 4 insider filings for watchlist
Job posting proxy for watchlist companies
USPTO patent filings by watchlist companies

chains.conclusion: Corporate chain shows elevated insider activity. SEC Form 4 insider filings for the watchlist (NVIDIA, Google, Novo Nordisk, Eli Lilly, Moderna, WeRide, Tempus AI, Schrodinger, Arvinas, Intellia, Beam, Denali, and others) surged 400% above the 30-day average with 5 filings, suggesting executive confidence or portfolio rebalancing ahead of potential announcements. SEC 8-K filings remain near baseline. Job postings and patent filings show no acceleration, indicating insider activity is not yet matched by hiring or IP expansion.

chains.name: Energy
chains.conclusion: Energy chain shows declining research activity. arXiv physics.app-ph preprints dropped 72% below the 30-day average, indicating a sharp pullback in applied physics research submissions relevant to energy technologies. ARPA-E press releases and commodity price data are unavailable. Crunchbase energy funding remains at baseline zero. The research slowdown may reflect seasonal patterns or a temporary lull in energy technology publication cycles.
